corpus.description: The canonical input to the competitive coverage gap-analysis program (backlog #315). An explicit, reproducible list of the leading design systems and UI libraries to benchmark, each with the metadata phase 2 (extraction) and phase 3 (mapping) join against. Membership is governed by the stated selectionCriteria below so a re-run lands on a comparable list. The references.json 'Reference Design Systems' set is the curated docs-surface subset of this corpus (flagged inReferences).

selectionCriteria.label: Adoption / influence
selectionCriteria.test: Widely used in production or widely copied by other systems (a pattern-setter, not a niche experiment).

selectionCriteria.label: Coverage breadth
selectionCriteria.test: Spans many component and pattern categories — a full system or primitive set, not a single-widget library.

selectionCriteria.label: Distinct philosophy / axis
selectionCriteria.test: Adds a perspective the corpus would otherwise miss (opinionated DS vs. headless behavior vs. web-component vs. enterprise-density vs. platform standard).

selectionCriteria.label: Native-platform alignment
selectionCriteria.test: Tracks web platform standards, WAI-ARIA APG, and native elements closely (informs whether a capability is a real gap or already native).

selectionCriteria.label: Public documentation / standards quality
selectionCriteria.test: Has browsable, citable per-component docs or guidelines — the source material the extraction phase reads.

selectionCriteria.label: Maintenance / currency
selectionCriteria.test: Actively maintained — a recent release or living standard, not abandoned.

corpus.inclusionRule: A source qualifies if it scores on at least three criteria AND occupies a category axis below. The corpus deliberately spans all axes rather than over-weighting the most popular names, so the gap sweep is not biased toward one design philosophy.

categories.label: Mature opinionated design system
categories.axis: Full, opinionated system with tokens, components, and usage guidance.

categories.label: Headless / primitive / a11y-first library
categories.axis: Unstyled behavior + accessibility primitives; the WAI-ARIA APG made code.

categories.label: Web-component / framework-agnostic system
categories.axis: Ships standards-based custom elements usable in any framework.

categories.label: Popular styled component kit
categories.axis: Batteries-included styled components for a specific framework ecosystem.

categories.label: Platform guideline / standards body
categories.axis: Native-platform or standards guidance (not shipped code) — the native-first anchor.

sources.name: Material Design 3
sources.philosophy: Opinionated cross-platform system; strong state, elevation, and motion specs.

sources.name: Carbon Design System
sources.philosophy: Enterprise data density, loading patterns, strict accessibility.

sources.name: Fluent 2
sources.philosophy: Productivity-focused, density controls, deep keyboard interaction.

sources.name: Spectrum
sources.philosophy: Adaptive scaling (touch/mouse), rigorous theming.

sources.name: Polaris
sources.philosophy: Commerce-admin system; strong content/UX guidance and patterns.

sources.name: Atlassian Design System
sources.philosophy: Productivity-app system; mature interaction and content standards.

sources.name: Primer
sources.philosophy: Developer-tool system; ViewComponent + React + CSS, a11y-forward.

sources.name: Lightning Design System
sources.philosophy: Enterprise CRM system; dense data, blueprints, strong a11y guidance.

sources.name: Radix UI Primitives
sources.philosophy: Modern headless behavior + ARIA management; the de-facto primitive set.

sources.name: React Aria
sources.philosophy: Behavior + a11y hooks distilled from APG; deep i18n and interaction coverage.

sources.name: Base UI
sources.philosophy: Unstyled accessible components from the MUI/Radix/Floating UI authors.

sources.name: Ark UI
sources.philosophy: State-machine-driven headless components across React/Vue/Solid.

sources.name: Headless UI
sources.philosophy: Minimal unstyled accessible widgets paired with Tailwind.

sources.name: Shoelace / Web Awesome
sources.philosophy: Framework-agnostic web components with a full component set.

sources.name: Spectrum Web Components
sources.philosophy: Spectrum delivered as standards-based custom elements.

sources.name: FAST
sources.philosophy: Web-component primitives and tooling underpinning Fluent web components.
sources.retiredReason: FAST removed its component library (fast-foundation/fast-components); packages/ now ships only fast-element/fast-html/fast-router tooling and the docs at fast.design 404. Its former components folded into Fluent UI Web Components (the fluent-2 source). No longer a web-component system and has 0 capability-presence rows. Retired per backlog #546 (axis still covered by shoelace / spectrum-web-components / lion).

sources.name: Lion
sources.philosophy: White-label accessible web-component primitives meant to be extended.

sources.name: Material UI (MUI)
sources.philosophy: Most-adopted React component kit; broad coverage, Material-based.

sources.name: Ant Design
sources.philosophy: Enterprise React kit; very wide component + data-entry coverage.

sources.name: Chakra UI
sources.philosophy: Style-props React kit emphasizing accessibility and theming.

sources.name: Mantine
sources.philosophy: Large React kit with rich hooks and form/data components.

sources.name: shadcn/ui
sources.philosophy: Copy-in components over Radix + Tailwind; the current default React pattern.

sources.name: Human Interface Guidelines
sources.philosophy: Defines the native-feel baseline for modal, navigation, and feedback.

sources.name: GOV.UK Design System
sources.philosophy: Gold standard for form validation, error messaging, and progressive enhancement.

sources.name: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
sources.philosophy: The canonical accessible-widget pattern + keyboard-interaction reference.

sources.name: Open UI
sources.philosophy: Standardizing native UI controls (selectlist/popover/etc.); the native-first frontier.

capabilities.description: Phase 2 of the gap-analysis program (backlog #346): the normalized capability matrix extracted from the benchmark corpus (#316). Each row is one capability the corpus exposes, deduped across sources into a stable id (e.g. Material 'Menu', Fluent 'Menu', Radix 'Dropdown Menu' -> one `menu` capability). Phase 3 (#347) joins each row to a Web Everything entity and labels coverage. This is a FIRST PASS keyed for diffability, not an exhaustive per-source enumeration (see method.scope).

method.howRead: Each source's component catalogue + documented patterns + accessibility/standards guidance were read and collapsed bottom-up into shared capability ids. A 'component' is a documented, named UI building block; a 'pattern' is a cross-cutting behavior a component composes (focus return, type-ahead, async pagination); a 'token' is a themeable design value; a 'standard' is an accessibility/i18n conformance expectation.
method.dedup: Capabilities are capability-keyed, not source-keyed: one id carries the union of sources that provide it. Synonyms are merged (snackbar = toast; sheet = drawer; combobox = autocomplete-select).

ubiquityScale.universal: present in essentially every implementing source (the component kits, mature DS, and most headless sets)
ubiquityScale.partial: present in a minority / only some axes
ubiquityScale.emerging: appearing recently or only in standards/native frontier

method.scope: FIRST PASS. `ubiquity` is a coarse cross-corpus signal and `notableIn` flags standout/canonical implementations; exact per-source presence + deep doc URLs per (capability x source) are deferred to the per-source fan-out (a #346 split under #315). `nativeAnchorHint` is recorded now so phase 3's native-first weighing has input.
method.reproducibility: Keyed by stable capability `id` so a re-run yields a clean diff (capabilities added / removed / re-classified) rather than a rewrite. Bump `version` + `lastSwept` per run.

kinds.label: Component
kinds.test: A documented, named UI building block.

kinds.label: Pattern
kinds.test: A cross-cutting behavior a component composes.

capabilities.label: Button
capabilities.summary: Primary/secondary/tertiary action triggers with variants and states.

capabilities.label: Icon button
capabilities.summary: Compact icon-only action with required accessible name.

capabilities.label: Button / toggle group
capabilities.summary: Segmented set of related actions or single/multi toggles.

capabilities.label: Split button
capabilities.summary: Default action + attached menu of alternatives.

capabilities.label: Text field
capabilities.summary: Single-line text input with label, hint, error.

capabilities.label: Number / stepper input
capabilities.summary: Numeric entry with increment/decrement and clamping.

capabilities.label: Select (single)
capabilities.summary: Single-choice picker from a list of options.

capabilities.label: Combobox / autocomplete
capabilities.summary: Text input filtering a popup list of suggestions.

capabilities.label: Radio group
capabilities.summary: Single choice among mutually exclusive options.

capabilities.label: Slider
capabilities.summary: Value selection along a track, single thumb.

capabilities.label: File upload / dropzone
capabilities.summary: File selection by picker and/or drag-and-drop.

capabilities.label: Form layout / field
capabilities.summary: Field wrapper composing label, hint, error, required.

capabilities.label: Disclosure / collapsible
capabilities.summary: Single show/hide region toggled by a trigger.

capabilities.label: Tabs
capabilities.summary: Switch among panels via a tab strip.

capabilities.label: Menu / dropdown menu
capabilities.summary: Action menu invoked from a button.

capabilities.label: Tree select
capabilities.summary: Selection from a hierarchical tree in a popup.

capabilities.label: Pagination
capabilities.summary: Page-by-page navigation of a result set.

capabilities.label: Popover
capabilities.summary: Non-modal anchored overlay with arbitrary content.

capabilities.label: Data table / data grid
capabilities.summary: Tabular data with sort, select, expand, edit.

capabilities.label: Empty state
capabilities.summary: Guidance shown when a region has no data.

capabilities.label: Anchored positioning
capabilities.summary: Placing an overlay relative to a trigger with collision handling.

capabilities.label: Focus trap
capabilities.summary: Containing focus within a modal surface.

capabilities.label: Focus return / restoration
capabilities.summary: Returning focus to the invoker on dismiss.

capabilities.label: Roving tabindex / arrow nav
capabilities.summary: Single tab stop with arrow-key movement in a composite.

capabilities.label: Type-ahead find
capabilities.summary: Jump to an item by typing its prefix.

capabilities.label: Dismissal (esc / outside-click)
capabilities.summary: Closing overlays via escape, outside click, scroll.

capabilities.label: Virtualization / windowing
capabilities.summary: Rendering only visible rows of large collections.

capabilities.label: Controlled / uncontrolled state
capabilities.summary: Component state owned externally or internally.

capabilities.label: Live-region announcements
capabilities.summary: Announcing async status changes to AT.

capabilities.label: Form validation + error summary
capabilities.summary: Field + summary error display, focus management, messaging.

capabilities.label: Copy to clipboard
capabilities.summary: Copy a value with confirmation feedback.

coverage.description: Phase 3 of the gap-analysis program (backlog #347): each extracted capability (#346) joined to a Web Everything entity and labelled covered / partial / missing / native-covered, weighed native-first. Partial/missing rows carry the gap-# triage (nativeFirst / gap / effort / rank) and an alreadyTracked flag (deduped against the backlog). The `gaps` array — un-tracked partial/missing rows — is the direct input to phase 4 (#348), which files them as candidate backlog items.

labels.covered: A WE entity (or a native primitive WE defers to) fully provides the capability.
labels.native-covered: The web platform provides it natively and WE intentionally defers to the native primitive (native-first).
labels.partial: A WE entity addresses the capability but misses dimensions, states, or an implementation block.
labels.missing: No WE entity and no native primitive covers it.

method.join: Each capability id resolved against blocks/intents/plugs/protocols/intents and the native platform. nativeAnchorHint from #346 promoted to the native-first weighing: a capability the platform provides natively is NOT a gap even if no WE block wraps it.
method.dedup: Before a row is called a fileable gap it is reconciled against existing backlog items (open + parked). alreadyTracked records the #NNN so phase 4 does not re-open settled work.
method.scope: Coverage labels reflect the first-pass capability matrix (#346); they refine as per-source extraction (#352) sharpens the matrix.

summary.note: fileableGaps: 11 at phase-3 -> 7 at phase-4 file-time dedup (menu+context-menu #173/#300, command-palette #299/#016, accordion #008) -> 6 when fill-time review found range-slider already covered by block:slider's dual-thumb range variant (#361). 2026-06-20 re-run: the 2 standing fileable gaps closed by resolved WE work and re-kinded partial->covered — date-picker by block:date-picker/datetime-picker/date-range-picker + intent:temporal (#359, resolved 2026-06-15) and design-tokens-theming by project:webtheme (#364, resolved). fileableGaps -> 0; 0 new items filed (idempotent). Corpus + capability matrix unchanged this run. The sweep self-corrects: dedup, fill-verification, and resolved-work re-kinding all demote false/closed gaps.

coveredHighlights.we: block:dropdown / droplist (+ native select, Open UI selectlist)
block:drawer (concrete member of the dialog family, #360)
block:notification (implements feedback intent; toast is a presentation style, #358)
block:slider (dual-thumb range — already specified; impl is Frontier UI's, #361)
block:date-picker / datetime-picker / date-range-picker / temporal + intent:temporal (#359 resolved 2026-06-15; was gap-3, closed since last sweep)
project:webtheme — DTCG-to-native-CSS token system (#364 resolved, graduatedTo project:webtheme; was gap-4)

alreadyTracked.note: dropdown async pagination paradigm tracked in #018.
DnD paradigms tracked in #022; intents cover the axis.
menu/menubar block tracked in #173 + #300 (standard); context-menu in #300 too.
context-menu folded into the menu/menubar/context-menu block standard #300.
command intent + invocation tracked in #299 / webcommands #016.
disclosure intent (gap-12) tracked in #008; accordion is a thin composition over it + native details.

coverage.skippedReason: Low value, a pure styling idiom, native-covered, folded into a larger filed gap (spacing/radius/elevation -> design-tokens-theming; contrast -> #010; tree-view -> #064), or composed trivially from existing entities. Logged here rather than filed so the omission is explicit, not silent.
